summary: Executed backend pytest regression (10 tests) and public-URL UI integration flow for PDF imports, reconciliation, multi-currency metrics, and CSV export. Core requested flows are working.

critical.issue: Latent bug from code review: pdf_extraction_service.build_prompt() returns invoice prompt unconditionally; scanned bank-statement OCR fallback can receive wrong extraction schema.

action_items: Fix build_prompt() in /app/backend/pdf_extraction_service.py with explicit dataset == 'bank' branch so scanned bank PDFs use the bank-statement JSON schema prompt.
After fix, retest scanned bank PDF OCR fallback path (API + UI if fixture available) to confirm no wrong-schema extraction.
critical_code_review_comments: In /app/backend/pdf_extraction_service.py, build_prompt() contains an early return and an unreachable second return block; bank prompt logic is never reached.
This issue is masked for text PDFs because heuristic extraction often succeeds before OCR fallback.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest passes 10/10 including fixture-based text/scanned PDF tests. Public URL UI flow passed for import -> auto-reconciliation -> MAD/EUR metrics -> export trigger. Remaining risk area is scanned bank PDF OCR fallback due prompt-branch bug in code.
rca of the issue: RCA: build_prompt() currently always returns invoice-oriented instructions because the bank-statement prompt block is unreachable. Reproduction path: submit scanned bank PDF where local text heuristic fails, forcing OCR fallback. The LLM may return invoice-shaped rows, causing parsing failures or degraded extraction for /api/reconciliation/import/bank. Mitigation: split build_prompt() into explicit invoices/bank branches and add unit/API regression for scanned bank OCR fallback.
